title: Day 4 · Safe Edit
subtitle: This map focuses on Day 4's control flow: the harness gates writes, read-before-edit consumes Day 3 state, and confirmed edits snapshot old content first.

diagrams.label: Write gate
diagrams.title: The harness gate before any write
diagrams.summary: The model can only propose a file_write / file_edit tool_use; the agent.py intercept block owns path boundaries, read state, diff preview, and y/N confirmation.

nodes.label: tool_use
nodes.detail: The model only states edit intent: which file_path, content, or old_string/new_string. It never writes directly to disk.

nodes.label: agent.py intercept block
nodes.detail: This is Day 4's key boundary: every write tool is intercepted, checked, diffed, and confirmed before it reaches ToolRegistry.run.

nodes.label: resolve_in_cwd
nodes.detail: Write tools reuse the same cwd boundary as read tools. A path that escapes cwd becomes an error ToolResult.

nodes.label: read-before-edit
nodes.detail: file_write on existing files and every file_edit must have been read first; if disk mtime changed after that read, check_mtime_conflict blocks the write.

nodes.label: apply_single_replace
nodes.detail: file_edit dry-runs replacement in memory. Empty old_string, no match, multiple matches, or old==new all return an error before any diff is shown.

nodes.label: render_diff
nodes.detail: The diff is for the human, not the messages list. The model only sees the eventual ToolResult observation.

nodes.label: typer.confirm
nodes.detail: The default is N. Pressing n returns error: edit rejected by user; pressing y allows real tool execution.

nodes.label: ToolRegistry.run
nodes.detail: Only after all gates pass and the user confirms does the harness call the original file_write / file_edit function.

nodes.label: ToolResult
nodes.detail: Path escape, unread file, mtime conflict, replacement failure, and user rejection all return as is_error=True observations.

diagrams.label: Read before edit
diagrams.title: How ReadFileState becomes a write gate
diagrams.summary: Day 3 only recorded ReadFileState; Day 4 reads it before writes to prove the model saw the target file and the file has not changed externally.

nodes.label: read_file
nodes.detail: The model usually calls read_file to inspect the target. Day 3 already ended that tool with ctx.read_state.record(path, content).

nodes.label: record(path, content)
nodes.detail: ReadFileState stores mtime_ns and character count only. The teaching version does not cache full content or use a content-equals fallback.

nodes.label: ReadFileState.entries
nodes.detail: This in-memory state is the evidence for read-before-edit. ToolContext is recreated for each agent-code invocation, so the state is per session.

nodes.label: write tool_use
nodes.detail: When the model later asks to edit an existing file, the harness checks ReadFileState instead of trusting the model's memory.

nodes.label: ensure_read_before_edit
nodes.detail: If the file has not been read, the harness returns an error ToolResult and skips both diff rendering and y/N confirmation.

nodes.label: check_mtime_conflict
nodes.detail: If disk mtime changed after the read, the harness reports a conflict and nudges the model to read_file again. This avoids patching stale content.

nodes.label: render_diff
nodes.detail: Only after read-before-edit and mtime gates pass does the harness compute new_content and show the diff.

diagrams.label: Backup safety net
diagrams.title: How old content enters .agent/history after confirmation
diagrams.summary: Backup happens inside the tool function, before write_text. It is not a model-visible tool, and backup failure does not block an edit the user already confirmed.

nodes.label: typer.confirm = y
nodes.detail: After the human reviews the diff and presses y, agent.py lets the request reach the real tool function.

nodes.label: file_write / file_edit
nodes.detail: file_write backs up only when the target already exists; file_edit backs up immediately after reading the old content.

nodes.label: file_history.backup
nodes.detail: backup is a harness-wide safety net. It is never registered in ToolRegistry, so the model cannot call it or know it exists.

nodes.label: .agent/history/<rel>/<ts>
nodes.detail: Snapshots are organized by relative file path and UTC millisecond timestamps, which makes later restore or retention-policy exercises straightforward.

nodes.label: Path.write_text
nodes.detail: Backup failures are swallowed so they do not block an edit the user already approved. The tool function still performs the actual write.

nodes.label: read_state.record
nodes.detail: After a successful write, read_state is refreshed so later edits in the same run are based on the latest file version.

nodes.label: ToolResult
nodes.detail: The model still sees only the normal tool result, such as Wrote 18 chars or Edited hello.txt. Backup details never enter the observation.
